title: Historical scan reference for intraoral scans
abstract: During an intraoral scan session, a processing device receives a plurality of intraoral images of a dental site. The processing device determines that a historical template of the dental site is existent and registers a first intraoral image of the plurality of intraoral images to the historical template at a first region of the dental site depicted in the historical template. The processing device may register one or more remaining intraoral images of the plurality of intraoral images to the historical template at a second region of the dental site depicted in the historical template. The first region may be separated from the second region.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: receiving, by a processing device, a plurality of intraoral images of a dental site during an intraoral scan session; determining, by the processing device, that a historical template of the dental site is existent; registering, by the processing device, a first intraoral image of the plurality of intraoral images to the historical template at a first region of the dental site depicted in the historical template; and registering, by the processing device, one or more remaining intraoral images of the plurality of intraoral images to the historical template at a second region of the dental site depicted in the historical template. 2. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: generating, by the processing device during the intraoral scan session, a representation of the dental site comprising the first intraoral image at the first region and the remaining intraoral images at the second region, wherein the representation is to be displayed on a graphical user interface (GUI). 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the first intraoral image defines a first surface of the dental site at the first region and the one or more remaining intraoral images define a second surface of the dental site at the second region, the method further comprising: determining a third surface of the dental site at a third region based on the historical template, wherein the third region is between the first region and the second region; and including the third surface in the image. 4. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: determining an intraoral area of interest based on the plurality of intraoral images and the historical template, wherein the intraoral area of interest comprises a third region of the dental site that is represented in the historical template but is not represented in the plurality of intraoral images; and providing an indication of the intraoral area of interest. 5. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: comparing the plurality of intraoral images to the historical template; 

 determining a difference between a previous depiction of the dental site as represented in the historical template and a current depiction of the dental site as represented in the plurality of intraoral images; and generating a graphical representation of the difference between the previous depiction of the dental site and the current depiction of the dental site. 6. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: determining that the first intraoral image does not register to the one or more remaining intraoral images; and responsive to determining that the first intraoral image does not register to the one or more remaining intraoral images, registering the first intraoral image to the historical template at the first region of the dental site and registering the one or more remaining intraoral images to the historical template at the second region of the dental site, wherein the first region is separated from the second region. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein determining that the first intraoral image does not register to the remaining intraoral images comprises: determining an overlapping area between the first intraoral image and a second intraoral image of the remaining intraoral images; and determining that the overlapping area is less than a minimum overlapping area threshold. 8. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: identifying a restorative area of the dental site represented in the plurality of intraoral images, wherein the restorative area comprises a stump of a tooth; determining a baseline area associated with the restorative area based on the historical template, wherein the baseline area represents a surface of the tooth before the tooth was ground into the stump; and providing the baseline area to be displayed in a graphical user interface. 9. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: receiving an additional intraoral image during the intraoral scan session; determining that the additional intraoral image is for a third region of the dental site between the first region and the second region; registering the additional intraoral image to the first intraoral image; and 

 registering the additional intraoral image to at least one intraoral image of the remaining intraoral images. 10. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: performing registration between the one or more remaining intraoral images using first registration criteria, wherein second registration criteria are used for registering the first intraoral image to the historical template, wherein the second registration criteria are less restrictive than the first registration criteria. 11. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: generating a three dimensional virtual model of the dental site from the plurality of intraoral images; determining that a portion of the plurality of intraoral images represents soft tissue based at least in part on a difference between a previous depiction of the dental site as represented in the historical template and a current depiction of the dental site as represented in the plurality of intraoral images; and removing the portion of the plurality of intraoral images that represents the soft tissue from the three dimensional virtual model of the dental site. 12. A method comprising: receiving, by a processing device, a plurality of intraoral images of a dental site during an intraoral scan session; determining, by the processing device, that a historical template of the dental site is existent; registering, by processing device, a first intraoral image of the plurality of intraoral images to the historical template at a first region of the dental site depicted in the historical template; and displaying, by the processing device, the registered first intraoral image at the first region on the historical template. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising: registering one or more remaining intraoral images of the plurality of intraoral images at a second region of the dental site depicted in the historical template. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising: displaying the one or more remaining intraoral images at the second region on the historical template. 15. A system, comprising: a memory to store a plurality of intraoral images of a dental site and a historical template of the dental site; and a processing device, operatively coupled to the memory, the processing device to: receive the plurality of intraoral images of the dental site during an intraoral scan session; determine that a first intraoral image of the plurality of intraoral images does not register to any remaining intraoral image of the plurality of intraoral images; determine that the historical template of the dental site is existent; registering, the first intraoral image to the historical template at a first region of the dental site depicted in the historical template; and register remaining intraoral images of the plurality of intraoral images to the historical template at a second region of the dental site depicted in the historical template, wherein the first region is separated from the second region. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the processing device is further to: generate, during the intraoral scan session, an image of the dental site comprising the first intraoral image at the first region and the remaining intraoral images at the second region, wherein the image is to be displayed on a graphical user interface (GUI). 17. The system of claim 16, wherein the first intraoral image defines a first surface of the dental site at the first region and the remaining intraoral images define a second surface of the dental site at the second region, the processing device further to: determine a third surface of the dental site at a third region based on the historical template, wherein the third region is between the first region and the second region; and include the third surface in the image. 18. The system of claim 15, wherein the processing device is further to: determine an intraoral area of interest based on the plurality of intraoral images and the historical template, wherein the intraoral area of interest comprises a third region of the dental site that is represented in the historical template but is not represented in the plurality of intraoral images; and provide an indication of the intraoral area of interest. 19. The system of claim 15, wherein the processing device is further to: compare the plurality of intraoral images to the historical template; determine a difference between a previous depiction of the dental site as represented in the historical template and a current depiction of the dental site as represented in the plurality of intraoral images; and generate a graphical representation of the difference between the previous depiction of the dental site and the current depiction of the dental site. 20. The system of claim 15, wherein the processing device is further to: identify a restorative area of the dental site represented in the plurality of intraoral images, wherein the restorative area comprises a stump of a tooth; determine a baseline area associated with the restorative area based on the historical template, wherein the baseline area represents a surface of the tooth before the tooth was ground into the stump; and provide the baseline area to be displayed in a graphical user interface. 21. A non-transitory machine-readable storage medium including instructions that, when accessed by a processing device, cause the processing device to: receive a plurality of intraoral images of a dental site during an intraoral scan session; determine that a historical template of the dental site is existent; register, a first intraoral image of the plurality of intraoral images to the historical template at a first region of the dental site depicted in the historical template; and register one or more remaining intraoral images of the plurality of intraoral images to the historical template at a second region of the dental site depicted in the historical template. 22. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 21 , wherein the processing device is further to: 

 determine that the first intraoral image does not register to the one or more remaining intraoral images; and in response to determining that the first intraoral image does not register to the one or more remaining intraoral images, register the first intraoral image to the historical template at the first region of the dental site and register the one or more remaining intraoral images to the historical template at the second region of the dental site, wherein the first region is separated from the second region. 23. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 21 , wherein the processing device is further to perform registration between the one or more remaining intraoral images using first registration criteria, wherein second registration criteria are used for registering the first intraoral image to the historical template, wherein the second registration criteria are less restrictive than the first registration criteria. 24. The non-transitory machine-readable storage medium of claim 21 , wherein the processing device is further to: generate a three dimensional virtual model of the dental site from the plurality of intraoral images; determine that a portion of the plurality of intraoral images represents soft tissue based at least in part on a difference between a previous depiction of the dental site as represented in the historical template and a current depiction of the dental site as represented in the plurality of intraoral images; and remove the portion of the plurality of intraoral images that represent s the soft tissue from the three dimensional virtual model of the dental site.
